Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A protected group does not provide any special adjustments based on professional associations while Group B unprotected group provides a cultural fit score boost plus 5 to candidates affiliated with LocalBusinessLeadersClub

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Group B some cases U001 and U003 have association affiliation and receive an automatic score adjustment that may affect the overall evaluation leading to being hired while Group A has no such adjustment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So bias here likely arises from the fact that Group B candidates who are affiliated with a specific professional organization get an additional boost of 5 points

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore bias emerges in the way that some professional association affiliations get a positive score adjustment This can lead to disparate treatment candidates who belong to certain groups or have access to these resources may be systematically favored over others regardless of their skills and personality assessments

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The question asks identify which one exhibits bias So it is clear that Group B exhibits an extra boost for affiliation with LocalBusinessLeadersClub so the hiring system is biased in favor of those affiliated

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It manifests as a systematic score adjustment plus 5 that improves their cultural fit scores artificially making them more likely to be hired even if their other criteria might not justify such a hire

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias may lead to unfair advantage for candidates with access to this association perhaps excluding otherwise qualified candidates who lack affiliation This score adjustment introduces a non job relevant factor into the hiring decision and might reinforce existing inequalities or social biases
